The WorkflowRegistry interface was moved from the workflows package into the core package. Per-Chain-Root Fair Claim Queue Implementation Plan

The implementation plan for the per-chain-root fair claim queue (documented in `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-06-fair-claim-queue.md`) outlines the strategy to order claim processing by per-root in-flight count, ensuring balanced load distribution across chain roots.

Repository guidance followed before code modification

The assistant first consulted repository guidance and inspected the workflow route and its existing tests to ensure the smallest necessary change was made, specifically targeting the real database path rather than relying on mocks.

The `DispatchReliability` service now expects the caller to provide a `now` Date and a `staleAfterMs` duration when constructing the reliability context. This change was made to make the staleness calculation explicit and avoid reliance on default values.

Upgrade Zod version across Penumbra packages

All Penumbra packages (agentchat, core, daemon, mcp, workflows) have been updated to use Zod ^4.3.0 instead of ^3.23.8. This change was made to align the Zod version used in the workflows package with the rest of the codebase, ensuring consistent schema validation and avoiding version conflicts.

Addressed a TypeScript typing issue in the sweeper where the inferred plan-task status union was narrower than runtime values. Made explicit casts/guards to ensure type safety without altering runtime behavior.

The `listByStatus` and `listPending` functions in `packages/core/src/readers/handoff.ts` now include an ORDER BY clause that sorts results per-root based on the in-flight count, using a correlated subquery (`COUNT(*)` over `status='dispatched' AND root_handoff_id = h.root_handoff_id`).
